Feature.Description: Ardea travel-carriage orchestration over the KEYENCE PLC (KV COM+). Reports the carriage position and moves the carriage to a target, but only when the DENSO robot is at a movable pose (base or retract). The move writes target/speed/accel, raises the move-request bit, waits for completion, and always clears the request bit afterwards. Speed and acceleration are fixed in the motion configuration.

Command.Description: Move the travel carriage to the target given by StationId, but only if the robot is at the base or retract pose and the carriage is ready. Intermediate responses report the current position [mm] while moving; the request bit is always cleared when the command finishes.

Parameter.Description: Target station identifier. For now this is parsed as an integer millimetre position and used directly as the target (temporary; it will become a fixed station-number-to-millimetre mapping later).

Response.Description: The carriage position [mm] after the move completed.

IntermediateResponse.Description: The carriage position [mm] observed during the move.

Property.Description: The current travel-carriage position [mm] (signed 32-bit device D6010), streamed periodically to subscribers.

DefinedExecutionError.Description: The robot is at neither the base pose nor the retract pose, so the carriage may not move.
The StationId is not a valid integer, or the resulting target is outside the travel range.
The carriage is not in an operable state (move-complete signal off or still moving).
The PLC reports a carriage fault/alarm.
The move did not complete within the configured timeout.
Failed to connect to or communicate with the PLC over KV COM+.
Failed to read or write a PLC device.
Failed to connect to or communicate with the robot controller over b-CAP (pose check).
Failed to acquire the robot or read the current joint angles (pose check).
